scope: 0990fe6a..HEAD (21 authored commits, 201 files, upstream-merged commits excluded)

confirmed_findings.summary: qing_keju.2 uses `order_by` inside `random_character`, but `order_by` is only honored by `ordered_*` list effects; in a `random_*` list it is silently ignored, so the laureate is chosen uniformly at random instead of by ability.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When qing_keju.2 fires, the immediate block runs `random_character = { limit = {...} order_by = combined_stats_council_svalue save_scope_as = laureate }` (again at line 152 in the else branch). Because random_character ignores order_by (weighting is done via `weight`, and the mod's own proven ablest-picker in se_QING_COUNCIL.txt uses `ordered_character` for exactly this), the engine saves a RANDOM eligible courtier as scope:laureate rather than the highest-stat one. Result: the '狀元'/天子門生 laureate awarded jinshi, loyalty and (option b) a Grand Council seat is a random courtier, defeating the merit-selection design. Confirmed: grep of both oracle clones (Invictus, Terra-Indomita) and the base-game events shows `order_by` never co-occurs with any `random_*` list — only with `ordered_*` (e.g. events/annexation.txt:449 ordered_character/order_by; se_QING_COUNCIL.txt:128 ordered_character/order_by).

confirmed_findings.summary: In QING_council_autofill the `is_target_in_variable_list` membership filter (lines 125 and 133) is evaluated in the iterated CHARACTER scope, but the qing_council_members list lives on the COUNTRY — so it never matches and the loop re-picks the same top-skill courtier forever, leaving the council with only one seat.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: At game start qing_mechanics_on_actions calls QING_council_autofill. ordered_character (char scope) tests `NOT = { is_target_in_variable_list = { name = qing_council_members target = this } }`; since the list is stored on CHI (added via `employer = { add_to_variable_list }` at line 60 and iterated in country scope by recompute at line 179), the character has no such list, so the predicate is always false and NOT is always true. The highest-combined-stat courtier is selected every iteration; QING_council_seat_character's own guard (correctly employer-wrapped, line 58) no-ops the re-add, so seat_count stays 1 while the loop spins to its count=12 backstop. The Qing convenes a 1-member Grand Council instead of filling to the 5-seat cap — the same file wraps this identical test in `employer = { ... }` in seat/unseat (lines 58, 92), confirming the unqualified uses here are wrong.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Read se_QING_COUNCIL.txt: list added/iterated in country scope (60,179); seat/unseat wrap the test in employer={} (58,92); autofill (125,133) and while-limit any_character (121-126) omit the wrapper.

confirmed_findings.summary: qing_office.5.b grants qing_council_effective with duration=1095 (3 years), but QING_council_apply_band unconditionally removes qing_council_effective at its top on every governance pulse, so the player-chosen bonus is wiped within one pulse.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Player picks option 5.b ('Favour the Manchu bloc') expecting a temporary 3-year council edge. On the next QING_GOV_pulse (00_monthly_country.txt:74, throttled ~quarterly), QING_council_apply_band runs `remove_country_modifier = qing_council_effective` (se_QING_COUNCIL.txt:267) then re-adds it only if effectiveness>=66. Unless effectiveness already qualifies (in which case the option added nothing), the modifier is silently stripped, so the option's sole reward evaporates.
confirmed_findings.evidence: office_events.txt:356 add duration=1095; se_QING_COUNCIL.txt:267 unconditional remove in apply_band; apply_band called each pulse via QING_GOV_pulse (se_QING_GOVERNANCE.txt:321, 00_monthly_country.txt:74).

confirmed_findings.summary: The qing_ili.4 compromise option drops qing_xinjiang_control to a mid-range 60 and calls only QING_ili_apply_control_band, never QING_ili_apply_prov_band, so the qing_xinjiang_prov_secured province modifiers stamped earlier remain permanently on the map.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: The only path to qing_ili.4 is .1 frontier -> .2 strike (QING_ili_reconquest_win sets control=80 and calls QING_ili_apply_prov_band, stamping qing_xinjiang_prov_secured on every Dzungaria/Tarim province) -> .3 repudiate -> .4. Choosing the .4 compromise sets control=60 (mid-range, where apply_prov_band's else branch is supposed to CLEAR both bands) but only refreshes the country band; the prov band is never re-run, and the chain completes at stage 4 so nothing calls it again. Result: all Xinjiang provinces keep local_tax_modifier +0.10 / local_population_happiness +0.05 / local_monthly_civilization +0.02 forever, in violation of the module's stated 'exactly one band per province in step with the meter' invariant.

confirmed_findings.summary: The loyalist arc's second task qing_jppre_fan_sonno_joi gates on var:qing_jppre_reform_faction >= 40, but the counter can only reach 31 by the time this task is unlockable, so the entire loyalist road past qing_jppre_back_daimyo is permanently blocked.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Player takes the loyalist road: the only reform_faction sources reachable before fan_sonno_joi are qing_jppre_cautionary_tale on_completion (+8, missions:137), event qing_japan_preperry.1 option a (+8, events:48 — the max of the two options), and event qing_japan_preperry.2's QING_jppre_back_daimyo (+15, se:120 via events:94/103). Total max = 31 (< 40), and no task/event/pulse anywhere else raises reform_faction (confirmed by grepping common/ and events/). fan_sonno_joi's allow can never be satisfied, so its capstone qing_jppre_restoration is never reachable and the concrete release_subject/start_civil_war break never fires.

confirmed_findings.summary: The loyalist capstone qing_jppre_restoration gates on var:qing_jppre_reform_faction >= 70 AND var:qing_jppre_shogun_grip < 40, both of which are unreachable given the counters' ceilings/floors.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Even if fan_sonno_joi (line 189) could be reached, reform_faction tops out at 31 + fan's own +12 (QING_jppre_back_daimyo at missions:200/204) = 43, never 70; and shogun_grip starts at 70 (se:58) and is only lowered by two -3 nudges on the loyalist path (QING_jppre_back_daimyo at se:121, fired by event .2 and by fan_sonno_joi), giving a minimum of 64, never < 40. No other effect mutates shogun_grip downward (grep of common/+events/ confirms). The Restoration capstone can never be completed.

confirmed_findings.summary: QING_advisor_recruit's character-mark guard tests whether the FIELD is staffed (employer = { var:qing_advisor_$field$ = 1 }), not whether THIS character staffed it, so a rejected same-field recruit is still falsely marked as a serving advisor.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Giquel arrives (qing_roster.15) and recruits field=naval, setting qing_advisor_naval=1 and consuming a slot. Later William Lang arrives (qing_roster.20) calls QING_advisor_recruit{field=naval}: the employer-block guard NOT={has_variable=qing_advisor_naval} fails so no slot is consumed (LOG_fail), but the mark block at line 104 sees qing_advisor_naval=1 (set by Giquel) and marks Lang with qing_is_foreign_advisor=flag:naval + the qing_foreign_advisor modifier anyway. Dismissing Lang then runs QING_advisor_dismiss_field{field=naval}, decrementing qing_advisor_slots_used and removing qing_advisor_naval, releasing Giquel's still-serving slot and stripping the active country modifier. Slot accounting is corrupted.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Read se_QING_ADVISORS.txt lines 81-108; QING_advisor_recruit callers grep shows roster.15 (naval/france) and roster.20 (naval/britain) both recruit field=naval; QING_advisor_dismiss_field lines 127-136 decrements slots_used and removes qing_advisor_naval.

confirmed_findings.summary: In MIGRATION_border_friction_breaking_point the guard flag migr_found_irredentist_kin is SET on root (the sending country) but READ at the surrounding effect scope, which is the destination province — so the read never sees it.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When a settler-flipped province reaches influx>=5 and a kin-state IS found, `root = { set_variable = migr_found_irredentist_kin }` (line 318, country scope) stamps the flag on the sending country, but the sibling checks at lines 332/338 (`has_variable = migr_found_irredentist_kin`) run in province scope and never see it: the LOG_fail 'no kin-state to press an irredentist play' fires spuriously EVERY time a play actually launched, and `remove_variable` (line 338) never runs so the flag leaks permanently on the sending country. Grepped: migr_found_irredentist_kin appears only in se_MIGRATION.txt; nothing else reads the leaked country var, so no gameplay corruption, but the diagnostic guard is inverted and the var accumulates.

confirmed_findings.summary: The newly-added AI_remove_diplomatic_play teardown at the end of DIPLOMACY_trigger_diplomatic_play_war strips all play_* variables while pre-existing event callers still read them after the call returns.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: send_settlers.txt (unchanged by the diff) does `scope:diplomatic_play = { DIPLOMACY_trigger_diplomatic_play_war = yes; ... scope:diplomatic_play.var:play_target_area.state = { every_state_province = { add_claim=... } } }` (lines 326-330). The new teardown removes play_target_area (se_AI.txt:905) before line 328 executes, so `var:play_target_area` reads a removed variable, `.state` resolves to nothing, the every_state_province loop iterates zero provinces, and the newly-released country silently gets no home-state claims (plus an engine 'variable not found' error). diplomatic_play_events.txt:635-638 additionally now double-tears-down (calls trigger_war then AI_remove_diplomatic_play again on the same, already-cleared play).

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW substantive effect QING_customs_appoint_ig carries only a LOG_line and is missing the mandated LOG_enter/LOG_exit wiring, while every sibling effect in the same file (establish, build_house, sinicize, pulse) is fully wired.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When Robert Hart is appointed IG from qing_roster.8 (scope:figure = { QING_customs_appoint_ig = yes }), the effect mutates country state (establish, ig_holder var, +modifiers, build_house) but emits no enter/exit markers, so if the appointment path later breaks or double-fires, the se_LOG trace shows no entry/exit for it — violating the standing per-effect logging rule that its siblings honour.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Read of se_QING_CUSTOMS.txt lines 107-126: only LOG_line at 125, no LOG_enter/LOG_exit; siblings at 54/68, 82/96, 136/153, 161/202 all carry enter+exit.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW effect QING_customs_init has no LOG wiring at all, inconsistent with the wired init effects elsewhere in the suite (QING_GOV_init, QING_DECLINE_init, QING_selfstr_init all carry LOG_enter/LOG_exit).
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: If the customs counter seeding regresses (e.g. a variable name typo leaves efficiency unset), the se_LOG trace has no enter/exit for QING_customs_init to localise the fault, unlike GOV_init/DECLINE_init which bracket their seeding — a diagnostic gap the standing rule exists to prevent.
confirmed_findings.evidence: se_QING_CUSTOMS.txt:43-46 has zero LOG calls; grep shows QING_GOV_init (se_QING_GOVERNANCE.txt:32), QING_selfstr_init (se_QING_SELFSTR.txt:36) and QING_DECLINE_init both open with LOG_enter and close with LOG_exit.

confirmed_findings.summary: New per-pulse band-applier effects QING_GOV_apply_capacity_band / _integrity_band / _exam_ladder_band carry only LOG_line (no LOG_enter/LOG_exit).
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: These are NEW effects added in this diff and the standing se_LOG rule wants LOG_enter/LOG_exit. However the whole band-applier family in the sibling se_QING_DECLINE.txt (QING_DECLINE_apply_currency_band, _corruption_band, _sect_band, etc.) is LOG_line-only with no enter/exit, so this is a pre-existing, consistent convention for band-appliers rather than a real inconsistency; flagged minor per the band-applier exception. No runtime effect — logging depth only.

confirmed_findings.summary: New meter-update effects QING_GOV_update_integrity / _update_capacity / _update_exam_ladder carry no LOG_enter/LOG_exit (they emit logging only indirectly via the nested QING_DECLINE_nudge LOG_line).
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: These NEW effects perform real variable computation but have no entry/exit markers. The analogous sibling update effects in se_QING_DECLINE.txt (QING_DECLINE_update_reform_pressure, QING_DECLINE_update_currency_stress) follow the same no-enter/exit pattern, so this matches an established whole-family convention; note that other NEW effects in the SAME file (QING_GOV_init, QING_GOV_yamen_band_track, QING_GOV_shuyuan_band_track, QING_GOV_pulse) DO carry full LOG_enter/LOG_exit wiring. Logging-completeness only; no runtime failure.

confirmed_findings.summary: New effects QING_techtransfer_init, _fire_track, _warm_partner, _dispatch, _call_favor, _pay_concession, and _refuse_concession lack LOG_enter/LOG_exit, whereas the main verb QING_techtransfer_bargain (line 57) in the same file is fully wired — a sibling-wiring inconsistency violating the standing se_LOG rule.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When a tech-transfer bargain fires or a favor is called/paid/refused, the se_LOG trace shows the bargain's enter/exit but drops into these helpers with only a bare LOG_line (init has no log at all), so a mid-chain failure inside e.g. QING_techtransfer_pay_concession cannot be bracketed to its function during playtest debugging. QING_techtransfer_init in particular emits nothing, unlike sibling init effects (QING_selfstr_init, QING_DECLINE_init) which carry full enter/exit.

confirmed_findings.summary: New effect QING_rebellion_end carries only a LOG_line and lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while its paired sibling QING_rebellion_begin (line 150) in the same file has both — a direct sibling-wiring inconsistency under the standing se_LOG rule.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When a rising is suppressed (QING_rebellion_end called from qing_rebellion_events / QING_reform_endstate_heavenly_kingdom), the log shows an unmatched entry: rebellion_begin brackets its work with enter/exit but rebellion_end does not, so if remove_country_modifier of qing_rebel_$flavour$ misbehaves for a given flavour the failure is not bracketed to the function. QING_reform_endstate_seal (line 40) and QING_taiping_advance (line 138) share the same enter/exit omission.

confirmed_findings.summary: New helper effects QING_selfstr_advance (48), QING_selfstr_build (73), QING_selfstr_raise_fleet (175), and QING_selfstr_raise_evarmy_legion (320) lack LOG_enter/LOG_exit, whereas the QING_selfstr_found_* sibling effects in the same file are all fully wired with enter/exit — a sibling-wiring inconsistency under the standing se_LOG rule.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: These are invoked heavily (found_* effects call advance/build/raise_fleet; the mission tree, Napoleon, Japan, USCW, advisors, students trees all call QING_selfstr_advance). If a create_unit/add_building_level fails inside raise_fleet or build, the trace has no enter/exit bracket to localize it, unlike the found_* callers, degrading playtest debuggability. The band-appliers QING_selfstr_apply_band/_apply_hollow_band are LOG_line-only, matching the pre-existing QING_DECLINE_apply_*_band convention, so those are acceptable; the advance/build/raise helpers are the genuine inconsistency.

confirmed_findings.summary: ECON_LOG country/jobs/currency snapshots stage temp vars onto the current scope (this) with set_variable but read them back and print the tag via ROOT.MakeScope/ROOT.GetTag, yet these effects are invoked inside every_country={ECON_LOG_quarter=yes} (oa_wealth_changes.txt:194) where this is the iterated country but ROOT is the on_action root, not that country.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Running with -debug_mode, quarterly_trade_pulse iterates every_country and calls ECON_LOG_quarter; ECON_LOG_country_snapshot does set_variable ECON_LOG_tmp_treasury on the iterated country, then LOG_line reads ROOT.MakeScope.GetVariable('ECON_LOG_tmp_treasury') where ROOT is not that country, so the variable is unset there and every [SNAP]/[JOBS]/[CURR] trace line prints treasury=0/natl_wealth=0 with a wrong/empty [ROOT.GetTag], making the diagnostic trace useless. The sibling ECON_LOG_production_snapshot avoids this by writing to root={change_variable} and reading from ROOT, highlighting the inconsistency; debug-only, no gameplay impact.
confirmed_findings.evidence: oa_wealth_changes.txt:185-195 calls ECON_LOG_quarter inside every_country; se_ECON_LOG.txt snapshots use set_variable on this then ROOT.MakeScope to read

confirmed_findings.summary: GOODS_cache_industrialisation_bonus is a NEW scripted_effect (per-quarter cache applier) carrying only LOG_line, with no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, violating the standing se_LOG enter/exit rule for new effects.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When debugging a wrong quarterly industrial-production number under -debug_mode, the trace shows the single 'cached industrialisation bonus' LOG_line but no ENTER/EXIT bracket for this effect, so its execution boundary/abort cannot be traced. No sibling effect in se_GOODS.txt carries any LOG wiring (this is the only logged effect in the file), so the sparse convention is pre-existing rather than a same-file inconsistency; hence minor.
confirmed_findings.evidence: grep of se_GOODS.txt shows exactly one LOG line (LOG_line at :18) and zero LOG_enter/LOG_exit across the whole file

confirmed_findings.summary: qing_office.4's trigger (line 239) and immediate (line 252) use the same unqualified `is_target_in_variable_list` in character scope against the country-held qing_council_members list, so already-seated councillors are not excluded.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Because the membership test in char scope always evaluates as 'not a member' (list lives on CHI, per seat_character's employer-wrapped guard), the event can fire and pick a courtier who is already on the council; option 4.a then spends 10 political influence and calls QING_council_seat_character, which no-ops the seating (its own guard is correct) while still running effects — a redundant offer and wasted PI. Same root cause as the autofill finding.
confirmed_findings.evidence: office_events.txt:239,252 unqualified test in any_character/random_character; contrast employer-wrapped guard in se_QING_COUNCIL.txt:58.

confirmed_findings.summary: qing_office.6.a (and qing_office.4 at line 255) apply `order_by` to `random_character`, which only honors `order_by` on `ordered_character`; the ordering is ignored and a uniformly random courtier is chosen.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: office.6.a's tooltip promises to 'appoint the ablest man to head' the Zongli Yamen, but random_character ignores order_by and picks any eligible courtier at random, so a mediocre character may be given the office instead of the highest combined_stats_council_svalue candidate. No engine error, but the stated 'ablest' selection is silently defeated.
confirmed_findings.evidence: office_events.txt:402-405 random_character{...order_by...}; correct pairing is ordered_character (used correctly at office.8 line 486 via ordered_in_list).

confirmed_findings.summary: The new council/office scripted_effects (QING_council_autofill, _recompute, _seat_character, _make_chief, _unseat_character, _apply_band, QING_office_appoint/_vacate/_vacate_dispatch) carry only LOG_line, not LOG_enter/LOG_exit, whereas sibling QING_council_grandee_flip in the same file is fully wired.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Standing se_LOG rule asks every new scripted_effect to bracket with LOG_enter/LOG_exit; these multi-step effects (autofill/recompute do real work) omit it, so a failure inside them cannot be traced by the enter/exit markers. Noted as minor: the wider QING suite convention is LOG_line-only for leaf/helper effects (e.g. se_QING_AFFINITY.txt has zero LOG_enter), with enter/exit reserved for pulse/roll entry points (QING_GOV_pulse, QING_roster_roll) and grandee_flip; the family here follows that established convention rather than a fresh inconsistency.
confirmed_findings.evidence: se_QING_COUNCIL.txt: only grandee_flip (322,350) has LOG_enter/LOG_exit; all other top-level effects have LOG_line only; se_QING_AFFINITY.txt grep LOG_enter = 0 (leaf-effect convention).

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW effect QING_napoleon_apply_conservative_backlash lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit (carries only a trailing LOG_line) while its file-siblings carry full wiring.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: On appointing Napoleon this effect iterates every staunch-Manchu councillor bleeding loyalty and nudges ethnic tension; with no entry/exit markers a failed every_character match (e.g. identity vars unseeded at early arrival) produces no bracketing log, contrary to the standing enter/exit rule the sibling effects honor.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Lines 120-156: single LOG_line at line 155, no LOG_enter/LOG_exit; contrast QING_gp_init/QING_selfstr_init/QING_DECLINE_init which all open with LOG_enter.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW init effect QING_napoleon_init has no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, unlike the fully-wired sibling init verbs QING_gp_init, QING_selfstr_init, and QING_DECLINE_init.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Fired from the mechanics startup hook to seed qing_napoleon_reforms_done; if seeding is skipped or mis-ordered there is no log entry to confirm the counter was initialized, breaking the enter/exit convention that all other *_init verbs in the suite follow.
confirmed_findings.evidence: se_QING_NAPOLEON.txt line 48-50 is a bare if/set_variable with no LOG calls; QING_gp_init (se_QING_DIPLO.txt:26) and QING_selfstr_init (se_QING_SELFSTR.txt:35) both wrap the same seeding pattern in LOG_enter/LOG_exit.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW per-pulse effect QING_napoleon_loyalty_pulse carries LOG_line/LOG_fail but no LOG_enter/LOG_exit; pulse-sibling convention is mixed (QING_DECLINE_pulse wired, QING_japan_pulse not).
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Runs every quarterly mechanics pulse; when Napoleon is absent it emits LOG_fail every pulse for the whole game with no enter/exit bracket, and when present logs only the branch taken — a per-pulse applier the standing rule says to report even though it carries LOG_line.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Lines 178-217: LOG_line in both loyalty branches and LOG_fail in the else, but no LOG_enter/LOG_exit; QING_DECLINE_pulse (se_QING_DECLINE.txt) opens with LOG_enter whereas QING_japan_pulse (se_QING_DIPLO.txt) does not.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW effect QING_COLON_clear_heartland_push carries only a LOG_line and no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, unlike its paired sibling QING_COLON_heartland_push.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When qing_frontier_migration_events calls QING_COLON_clear_heartland_push to lift the push, the log records only the cleared line with no enter/exit bracket; since its set-side pair (heartland_push) emits enter/exit, the clear-side trace is asymmetric and a no-op (no provinces had the var) is indistinguishable from a skipped call.
confirmed_findings.evidence: awk scan: QING_COLON_clear_heartland_push enter=0 exit=0 line=1 fail=0; sibling QING_COLON_heartland_push enter=1 exit=1

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW effect QING_jppre_deepen_intel carries only LOG_line, missing LOG_enter/LOG_exit.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Per the mod's standing se_LOG rule every new scripted_effect should wrap LOG_enter/LOG_exit; deepen_intel has only LOG_line. This matches its sibling lever-effect family in se_QING_DIPLO.txt (QING_japan_warm_accord/raise_rivalry/meiji_advance are all LOG_line-only, no enter/exit), so it is a pre-existing family convention rather than an inconsistency, but flagged for completeness.

confirmed_findings.summary: New large dispatcher QING_frontier_flavour_roll (~340 lines, ~40 event branches) carries no se_LOG calls at all (no LOG_enter/LOG_exit/LOG_line), while its sibling roll effects in the same file are fully wired.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: When the monthly Qing pulse fires the flavour roll and one branch mis-fires or an event id silently no-ops, the se_LOG trace shows entry into QING_DECLINE_pulse but no record that the flavour dispatcher ran or which branch it took, so a playtester diagnosing a missing/duplicate flavour event has no log breadcrumb — unlike QING_rebellion_roll (L1172) and QING_culture_roll (L1277), which both emit LOG_enter/LOG_exit.
confirmed_findings.evidence: awk/python per-effect scan of se_QING_DECLINE.txt: QING_frontier_flavour_roll (L831) enter=0 exit=0 line=0 fail=0; sibling QING_rebellion_roll (L1172) and QING_culture_roll (L1277) both enter=1 exit=1. Confirmed by reading L831-890 (random_list dispatching qing_reform.*/qing_treaty.*/qing_vassal.* with no LOG lines).

confirmed_findings.summary: The entire new CURR_STRESS_* effect family (nudge, update, classify, apply_generic_band, pulse) lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit; this is a consistent whole-file convention (no sibling in this file is wired), so it is a pre-existing-style convention note rather than an intra-file inconsistency.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: The generic monthly currency-stress pulse (CURR_STRESS_pulse, called from monthly_currency_pulse for non-CHI countries) produces no bounded se_LOG enter/exit trace, so if a non-CHI country's reserve-strain/crisis band fails to apply there is no log breadcrumb; noted as whole-family convention because no CURR_STRESS_* effect in the file is wired (unlike the QING files where entry-point effects are wired).
confirmed_findings.evidence: python per-effect scan of se_CURRENCY_STRESS.txt: CURR_STRESS_nudge (L35), CURR_STRESS_update (L51), CURR_STRESS_classify (L76), CURR_STRESS_apply_generic_band (L101), CURR_STRESS_pulse (L122) all enter=0 exit=0 — no sibling carries enter/exit anywhere in the file.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW wrapper SUBJ_QING_add_integration has no se_LOG instrumentation (delegates to advance_integration).
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: This backward-compat +1 wrapper emits no enter/exit of its own; a call that routes through it appears in the trace only as the inner advance_integration, hiding the wrapper hop. Tiny pure-macro wrapper (the inner effect it calls is fully logged), so minor, but still missing per the standing rule.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Read se_SUBJECT_QING.txt:324-326 — body is a single call to advance_integration, no LOG_*.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW effect MIGRATION_border_friction_breaking_point carries LOG_line/LOG_fail but no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while its siblings in the same file are fully wired.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: In -debug_mode, tracing a friction-driven diplomatic play launch: MIGRATION_try_emigrate_from_province, _on_arrival, _decay and _seed_claim_if_flipped all emit enter/exit bracketing, but this substantial effect does not, so its execution window cannot be bounded in the MIGR trace. Siblings wired => real inconsistency, not a whole-family convention.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW helper effects MIGRATION_border_friction_opinions (255) and MIGRATION_on_ownership_change (440) carry only LOG_line, no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while most siblings in the file are fully wired.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: In -debug_mode these two new effects emit only a single action line with no enter/exit; since the majority of effects in se_MIGRATION.txt (country_pulse, try_emigrate, on_arrival, decay, seed_claim) carry full enter/exit wiring, these are real inconsistencies per the standing LOG rule rather than a uniform file convention.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW effects DEJURE_on_ownership_change (70) and DEJURE_seed_dejure_claim (116) lack LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while sibling DEJURE_freeze_setup in the same file has full enter/exit wiring.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: In -debug_mode, tracing a conquest's de-jure disaffection/irredentist-claim path yields LOG_line/LOG_fail but no enter/exit bracketing for these two effects; because DEJURE_freeze_setup IS fully wired, this is a real intra-file inconsistency under the standing LOG rule.

confirmed_findings.summary: NEW effects CLAIM_HOSTILITY_on_transfer (44) and CLAIM_HOSTILITY_on_incursion (76) have no LOG_enter/LOG_exit (only LOG_line/LOG_fail).
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: In -debug_mode, tracing a claim-hostility reaction to a seizure/occupation, neither effect emits enter/exit so its execution window cannot be bounded; note the whole (new) file is uniformly LOG_line/LOG_fail-only with no wired sibling to contrast, so this reads as a self-consistent style within the file rather than a divergence, but still violates the standing enter/exit rule for NEW effects.

confirmed_findings.summary: Three NEW scripted_effects (SEPARATISM_try_secession line 88, SEPARATISM_spawn_breakaway line 140, SEPARATISM_liberate_across_borders line 368) carry only LOG_line/LOG_fail and lack the LOG_enter/LOG_exit pair the standing rule requires, while in-file siblings do carry it.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: During playtest a secession misfires (LAND_release_from_list yields no country, or the reconquest war never resolves). The SEPAR log shows LOG_enter/LOG_exit bracketing SEPARATISM_country_pulse and SEPARATISM_qing_pulse (lines 67/75, 503/531) but NO enter/exit trace for try_secession/spawn_breakaway/liberate, so the failing call cannot be localized by the enter/exit pairing the mod's debugging convention relies on.
confirmed_findings.evidence: grep 'LOG_enter\|LOG_exit' se_SEPARATISM.txt shows enter/exit only at lines 67/75, 503/531, 572/619 (the three pulse/spawn effects); try_secession/spawn_breakaway/liberate bodies have LOG_line+LOG_fail only.

confirmed_findings.summary: All 12 NEW scripted_effects in this new file (QING_char_affinity, QING_char_bind, nine char mutators, QING_pair_friction) carry only LOG_line and none call LOG_enter/LOG_exit, unlike the sibling new file se_QING_MISSIONARY.txt added in the same diff which fully wires enter/exit.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: If QING_char_affinity produces a bad score or a mutator (QING_char_taint/QING_char_assimilate) misbehaves during a Grand-Council/keju event, the QING log has no enter/exit trace to bracket the call, unlike the peer missionary file. The whole AFFINITY family is uniformly LOG_line-only (self-consistent within the file), but diverges from the mod norm and the sibling file in the same changeset.

confirmed_findings.summary: This diff rewrote every line ending to a doubled-CR sequence (\r\r\n); the base blob was clean CRLF, so all 1038 lines are now byte-corrupted.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: git cat-file -p HEAD:common/on_action/00_specific_from_code.txt shows 1038 '\r\r\n' sequences vs 0 in base 0990fe6a (which had 1007 clean '\r\n'); `file` reports mixed 'CRLF, CR line terminators'. The Clausewitz tokenizer treats CR as whitespace so the file still parses and braces balance (324/324), but the corruption bloats every future diff of this file and is a byte-convention regression introduced by this changeset.

confirmed_findings.summary: The two rewritten top-level effects SE_qing_armies (line 108) and SE_occupation_of_france (line 172) open with LOG_enter but neither closes with a LOG_exit, breaking the enter/exit pairing convention.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: grep shows LOG_enter=2, LOG_exit=0 in this file, whereas every sibling scripted-effect module pairs them exactly (se_SEPARATISM 3/3, se_MIGRATION 5/5, se_QING_DECLINE 9/9, se_SUBJECT_QING 5/5). In a -debug_mode playtest the debug.log trace will show 'ENTER SE_qing_armies' / 'ENTER SE_occupation_of_france' with no matching 'EXIT ... result=', so a hang or partial failure inside either game-start effect cannot be bounded from the log. Purely diagnostic; no gameplay effect. (The NEW per-item helpers SE_qing_raise_garrison[_cmd] correctly carry only LOG_line/LOG_fail, which is appropriate for a per-item applier.)

confirmed_findings.summary: New scripted_effects DIPLOMACY_resolve_get_territory (876), DIPLOMACY_resolve_influence (976), DIPLOMACY_resolve_subjugate (1011) and DIPLOMACY_seed_feature_play_success (172) carry only LOG_line/LOG_fail and omit the LOG_enter/LOG_exit the standing rule requires.
confirmed_findings.failure_scenario: Sibling effects touched in this same diff (DIPLOMACY_kickoff_play_event:354, DIPLOMACY_trigger_play_event:423, DIPLOMACY_add_state_foreign_influence:1047, DIPLOMACY_remove_state_foreign_influence:1138) all received full LOG_enter/LOG_exit wiring, so these four new effects are a genuine wiring inconsistency: when a play resolution misbehaves at playtest the trace shows the concrete action but no entry/exit envelope for the resolve helpers, breaking the enter/exit pairing the log framework relies on to bracket a failing call.

refuted.summary: NEW effect QING_students_init has no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, unlike the wired sibling init effects in the governance/decline/selfstr modules.
NEW effect QING_treaty_init has no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, inconsistent with the wired init effects used across the rest of the Qing suite.
NEW substantive effect QING_napoleon_take_office has no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, only one internal LOG_line, while every sibling effect in the file is fully wired.
NEW effect QING_COLON_frontier_pull has LOG_line + LOG_fail but no LOG_enter, unlike its wired sibling QING_COLON_heartland_push in the same file.
NEW pure-wrapper effect QING_COLON_frontier_pull_here (set_variable + LOG_line) lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit present on wired siblings.
NEW helper QING_MIGR_move_one_nomad emits LOG_line on both branches but no LOG_enter/LOG_exit; its parent QING_MIGR_nomad_ingather is fully wired.
NEW scripted_effect QING_ili_close_dungan carries only a LOG_line and no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, unlike its siblings in the same file.
NEW band-applier QING_ili_apply_control_band carries only LOG_line and no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while its direct sibling QING_ili_apply_prov_band in the same file has full enter/exit wiring.
NEW init effect QING_jppre_init carries no LOG calls at all (no enter/exit/line).

New branchy helper QING_regional_army_bind_commander (if/else with create_character + ordered_character) has only LOG_line, missing LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while its sole caller and neighboring complex effects in the same file are fully wired.

New guarded helper QING_reassert_strip_magnate (if/else with ordered_character iteration) carries only LOG_line/LOG_fail, missing LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while its caller QING_reassert_central_command is fully wired.

All NEW effects in se_QING_ADVISORS.txt (QING_advisor_recruit, QING_advisor_dismiss, etc.) carry LOG_line/LOG_fail but no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, whereas sibling suite files in this same diff do wire them.
The NEW effect DIPLOMACY_agitation_dejure_bridge carries LOG_line/LOG_fail but no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, unlike its se_DIPLOMACY siblings.
NEW effect SUBJ_QING_reduce_integration lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit though it carries LOG_line and LOG_fail.
NEW effect SUBJ_QING_suspend_integration lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit (has only LOG_line + LOG_fail).
NEW effect SUBJ_QING_resume_integration lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit (has only LOG_line + LOG_fail).
NEW dispatcher SUBJ_QING_roll_reaction lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit (has LOG_line + LOG_fail only).
NEW helper SUBJ_QING_clear_integration_state carries only LOG_line, no LOG_enter/LOG_exit.
NEW helper SUBJ_QING_derive_rung has no se_LOG instrumentation at all.

The PARTIAL get_territory path calls the singular LAND_transfer_province directly (lines 919 and 931) instead of the LAND_transfer_provinces wrapper, bypassing the mandatory post-transfer bookkeeping the wrapper performs.

In this new file only QING_gp_init (26) and QING_gp_scan_plays (610) carry LOG_enter/LOG_exit; ~34 substantive sibling verbs (e.g. QING_gp_react 207, QING_japan_warm_accord 96, QING_japan_raise_rivalry 113, QING_gp_frontier_play 361, QING_gp_partition 408, QING_gp_accommodate 448, QING_gp_court 488, QING_gp_apply_bands 519, QING_japan_pulse 144) carry only LOG_line/LOG_fail and lack the enter/exit pair.

NEW effects QING_embassy_init (64), QING_embassy_grant_trade (74, add_treasury + modifier + treaty-port grant) and all per-power wrappers (QING_embassy_britain_rebuff etc., 209-227) lack LOG_enter/LOG_exit, whereas the triangle/minor rebuff/receive siblings (103,126,153,171) in the same file are fully wired.

NEW effects QING_legation_init (38) and QING_legation_ease_tension (75, mutates qing_gp_tension_* / calls QING_japan_warm_accord) carry no LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while siblings QING_legation_open (50) and QING_legation_burlingame_mission (89) in the same file are fully wired.
NEW effect QING_roster_finalize (45) carries only a LOG_line and lacks LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while its sibling QING_roster_roll (67) in the same file is fully wired with enter/exit.

NEW effects QING_vassal_pressure_encroacher (163) and QING_vassal_apply_band (188) lack LOG_enter/LOG_exit, while the majority of siblings in the same file (QING_vassal_defend 53, _abandon 71, _free_real_subject 106, _sync_lost_flags 135, _pulse 175) are fully wired.
